questions.text: Does the main switch operating range correspond to the maximum current occurring in normal operation?
Is the main switch installed in the correct location?
Is there a main switch or emergency stop switch within < 1 m of the machine installation location?
Is there an installation switch or emergency stop within < 1 m of the emergency and test tableau(s)?
Is an emergency stop switch installed near the entrance of the pulley room, functional and appropriately labelled?
Is the light intensity in the machine room, at the machine and control installation locations, at least 200 lux on the floor?
Is the light intensity in the pulley room at least 100 lux at the pulleys?
Is a socket outlet per 13.6.2 available in the pulley room?
Is the power supply for the lighting of the car, control, shaft, machine and pulley room, and emergency tableau independent of the position of the installation switch?
Does each circuit controlled by switches have its own independent short-circuit protection?
Is the light intensity at the emergency and test equipment at least 50 lux and is a switch for this lighting provided?
Do the dimensions and safety clearances meet the minimum requirements?
Is the access safe, lit, direct and not routed through private areas?
Does the machine room ventilation comply with the layout plan?
Are the doors/hatches fitted with a conventional cylinder lock and interior turn knob?
Is closing and locking without a key guaranteed for an MRL control cabinet?
Are floor openings secured with collars of at least 50 mm height?
Is the machine room floor coated with laminate or oil-resistant paint, and does a threshold or oil catch prevent oil from leaking?
Is the machine room free of foreign installations?
Are all notices and signs in place?
For multiple lifts with a shared machine room, are all installation components identified according to their affiliation?
For a travel height > 30 m or where direct acoustic communication is not possible, is an intercom with emergency power supply available between the emergency intervention location and the car?
Are means for lifting heavy components available and marked with the permissible load capacity?

groups.title: 3.1 Safety Clearances at the Top of the Shaft

questions.text: Do the safety clearances comply with the standard requirements when the piston is in the uppermost position limited by the travel limiter, or the refuge space safety device is activated?
Is the standing area on the car roof at least 0.12 m² and is the smaller side length at least 250 mm?
Does the refuge space on the car roof correspond to a cuboid of at least 500 x 600 x 800 mm?
Does the remaining guide length of the counterweight correspond to the rated value when the car rests on the fully compressed buffers?
Is the final limit switch correctly positioned and functional?
Do the safety clearances comply with the standard requirements when the car rests on the fully compressed buffers?
Does the refuge space under the car correspond to a cuboid of at least 500 x 600 x 1000 mm?
For a reduced refuge space, does the safety device comply with the design examination in the lift book?
Is the distance between the car floor and the lowest landing less than 120 mm when the car with rated load rests on the buffers?
Do the installed car buffers comply with the specifications in the lift book?
(Energy accumulation buffers) Does the buffer stroke comply with the manufacturer's specifications when the car with rated load and slack ropes lands on the buffer?
(Energy dissipation buffers) Is damage to the lift ruled out when the car with rated load lands on the buffer?
Does the lift function correctly after the buffer test?
In a fully enclosed shaft enclosure, are only the permitted openings present?
Are flat or curved glass panels in the traffic area made of laminated safety glass?
Is a solid partition at the counterweight present?
Is the distance between counterweight and car at least 50 mm?
For lifts in a shared shaft, is a partition in the pit present (height up to 2.5 m above the lowest landing)?
Do the maintenance and emergency doors and maintenance hatches meet the requirements?
Does the access to the pit meet the requirements (ladder)?
Does the shaft ventilation comply with the layout plan specifications?
Does the shaft wall on the access side comply with the layout plan or standard specifications?
Are there no foreign installations in the shaft?
For accessible rooms below the pit, have appropriate protective measures been taken?
Are the necessary guards present at the rope pulleys and chain wheels?
Is the pit switch (emergency stop switch) correctly positioned, functional and appropriately labelled?
Is an emergency call device for rescue from the pit installed and functional?
Does the mechanical device for stopping the car during maintenance and inspection work function correctly?
Is the shaft illumination at least 50 lux at a height of 1000 mm above the car roof or pit floor?
Can the shaft lighting be switched on and off from both the pit access and near the main switch?
Is a socket outlet available in the pit and at the machine and control installation location?
Do the dimensions of the guide rails comply with the manufacturer's specifications?
Does the guide rail bracket spacing comply with the layout plan?
With the door closed, do the gaps between door panels or between panels and frame or sill not exceed 6 mm?
Do the recesses and projections on the outer side of landing doors not exceed 3 mm?
Do the landing doors correspond to the correct fire resistance class?
Are the correct door locks installed?
Have the contacts on each landing door been tested to ensure they lock on failure and prevent car movement outside the unlocking zone?
Do the locking elements engage at least 7 mm before the car can move?
Have the mechanical locks on each landing door been tested for correct locking?
Is the unlocking zone a maximum of 200 mm above and below the landing doors (or 350 mm for simultaneously operated car and landing doors)?
Does the automatic closing mechanism work correctly?
Do the door aprons meet the minimum requirements regarding length, chamfering, etc.?
Can each landing door be unlocked from the outside with an emergency key?
Is the illumination at the landing doors on the floor at least 50 lux?
Are prohibitory signs as per ISO 3864-1:2002 installed at the landings? (Do not use lift in case of fire)
Is information on rated load, manufacturer/installer name, number of persons and serial number present in the car?
Does the car emergency call device allow two-way communication with a duty service?
Does the car lighting achieve a minimum of 50 lux on the floor and at the controls?
Does the emergency lighting in the car work?
Does the overload protection function as required (at 10% overload but min. 75 kg)?
Is adequate ventilation provided in the car?
Are car walls, floor and ceiling solid, except for permitted ventilation openings?
Are the inspection control, emergency stop and socket outlet on the car roof correctly positioned and tested?
Does the emergency call device on the car roof work correctly?
With the car door closed, do the gaps between panels or between panels and frame not exceed 6 mm?
Do the recesses and projections on the inner side of car doors not exceed 3 mm?
Is the horizontal distance between landing door and car door sill a maximum of 35 mm?
Does the car door apron meet the minimum requirements regarding length, chamfering, etc.?
Is the maximum force required to prevent closing 150 N or less?
At an opening force of 150 N at the most unfavourable point of the closing edge, do the gaps not exceed 30 mm (single-side opening) or 45 mm (centre opening)?
Is the kinetic energy at average closing speed a maximum of 10 J?
Does a protective device reopen the door if a person is struck or could be struck in the door area?
Can the car door be opened manually within the unlocking zone with a maximum of 300 N when the power is switched off?
Have the contacts on the car door been tested to ensure they lock on failure and prevent car movement outside the unlocking zone?

groups.title: 6 Suspension / Protection Against Unintended Car Movement

questions.text: Do the number and nominal diameter of ropes match?
Are the correct rope ends installed and is load distribution between the ropes ensured?
Does the protection device against unintended car movement comply with the lift book specifications?
Does the protection device detect and prevent unintended car movement away from the landing when the shaft and car doors are not secured?
Are the required safety clearances maintained?
When the protection device is triggered, is the electrical safety device actuated?
Can releasing or resetting the lift after the protection device is triggered only be performed by a qualified person?
Is the correct safety gear installed?
(Instantaneous safety gear) Does the safety gear stop the car with rated load at rated speed?
(Progressive safety gear) Does the safety gear stop the car with rated load at rated speed?
When catching with uniformly distributed load, does the car floor tilt by a maximum of 5% from horizontal?
Does the electrical cut-off device function when the safety gear is triggered (drive is switched off)?
Does the lift function correctly after the safety gear test?
Is the correct overspeed governor installed?
Is the overspeed governor accessible or can it be tested and reset remotely?
Does the electrical safety component on the overspeed governor stop the lift?
Does the electrical monitoring of the governor rope function and stop the lift?

groups.title: 7.5 Pipe Rupture Valve — 7.6 Throttle Valve — 7.7 Anti-Creep Measures

questions.text: Is the correct pipe rupture valve installed?
Is a test device for the pipe rupture valve available?
Is the pipe rupture valve test successful? (Car stops at downward rated speed plus max. 0.3 m/s.)
Is the correct throttle valve installed?
Is a test device for the throttle valve available?
Is the throttle valve test successful? (Car speed < vmax + 0.3 m/s)
(Electrical anti-creep system) Does the electrical levelling correction system function at the latest when the car with rated load sinks 120 mm?
Does the car independently travel to the lowest landing at the latest 15 min after the last trip?
Were any electrical bypasses removed after the tests?
Were any changed settings restored to their original state after the tests?
Does the hydraulic power unit comply with the manufacturer's specifications?
Is a pressure gauge in the correct position available?
Is a hand pump with pressure relief valve available? (Lift with safety or clamping device)
Is a mains-independent position indicator (door unlocking zone) available and positioned so it is clearly visible during emergency evacuation?
Is the stopping accuracy of the car within ±10 mm?
Is a levelling correction of ±20 mm guaranteed for a levelling deviation of more than 20 mm?
With the car lowered, is the oil reservoir adequately filled and can the oil level be easily checked, refilled and drained?
Does the jack comply with the manufacturer's specifications?
Is the jack installed in the ground fitted with a protective tube?
Do the pipelines comply with the manufacturer's specifications?
Do the hose assemblies comply with the manufacturer's specifications?
Are the minimum bending radii of the hose assemblies maintained?
Is the measured speed with empty car at most 108% of the rated speed in the upward direction?
Is the measured speed with rated load at most 108% of the rated speed in the downward direction?
Has the NIV protocol (low-voltage protocol) been prepared?
Are all conditions of the NIV protocol fulfilled?
Are all guide rails and any shaft structure connected to the equipotential bonding?
Is it ensured that phase reversal cannot lead to a dangerous operating condition?
Has the safety circuit been tested to ensure that an earth fault triggers a shutdown? (See NIV protocol)
Do the circuits for inching and relevelling with open doors function correctly?
Is the motor running time monitoring correctly adjusted?
Does the oil temperature monitoring function correctly and does the lift travel to the next stop after the temperature sensor triggers?
Does the inspection control function correctly?
Does the ramp travel control function correctly?

groups.title: 11 Lift Behaviour in Case of Fire

questions.text: Is a fire recall control available?
Is the signal of the early fire detection and alarm system connected?
Can the fire recall control only be reset with the key switch?
Does a power interruption or control reset not cause the fire recall control to reset?
Is the fire recall key switch of bistable design and the key removable in each position?
Is the conventional key cylinder installed?
With an active fire signal, are all car call, landing call and 'DOOR OPEN' buttons inoperative?
With an active fire signal, are all stored travel commands deleted?
Do the automatic doors close and does the lift travel without interruption to the fire recall landing?
Are the inspection control and electric recall control not impaired by the fire detection system?
Is an operator's manual (lift book) available?
Is an operating manual available?
Are all parts related to the installation, for which the manufacturer/installer is not directly responsible, in a suitable condition for the lift to be commissioned?
Have all tests and inspections been carried out to prove compliance with the regulations?
Can the declaration of conformity be issued for this lift?
